info.description: The API makes generative AI models available, at the same credits prices listed here ($0.01 per credit): https://help.runwayml.com/hc/en-us/articles/15124877443219-How-do-credits-work

get.description: List avatars for the authenticated user with cursor-based pagination.

Python SDK

# pip install runwayml
from runwayml import RunwayML

# The env var RUNWAYML_API_SECRET is expected to contain your API key.
client = RunwayML()

for avatar in client.avatars.list():
    print(avatar)

# pip install runwayml
from runwayml import RunwayML

# The env var RUNWAYML_API_SECRET is expected to contain your API key.
client = RunwayML()

avatar = client.avatars.create(
  name='Customer Support Agent',
  reference_image='https://example.com/reference.jpg',
  personality='You are a helpful customer support agent. Be friendly and concise.',
  voice={ 'type': 'runway-live-preset', 'preset_id': 'adrian' },
)
print(avatar)

name.description: The character name for the avatar.

referenceImage.description: A HTTPS URL, Runway URI, or data URI containing the avatar reference image. See [our docs](/assets/inputs#images) for supported formats.

anyOf.description: A HTTPS URL.

get.description: List realtime avatar conversations for the authenticated user with cursor-based pagination. Each conversation corresponds to a realtime session, and the conversation ID matches the realtime session ID. Pass `avatar` to restrict results to a single avatar.

x-codeSamples.label: Node SDK
Python SDK
x-codeSamples.source:
# pip install runwayml
from runwayml import RunwayML

# The env var RUNWAYML_API_SECRET is expected to contain your API key.
client = RunwayML()

for conversation in client.avatar_conversations.list():
    avatar_name = conversation.avatar.name if conversation.avatar else None
    print(conversation.name, avatar_name, conversation.status)

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Cursor from a previous response for fetching the next page of results.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of items to return per page.

parameters.name: avatar
parameters.description: Filter to conversations that used the given custom avatar.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Filter conversations created on or after this timestamp (inclusive).

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Filter conversations created before this timestamp (exclusive).

items.title: ConversationListItem
items.description: Summary of a conversation for list responses.

id.description: Unique conversation identifier. This is the same value as the realtime session ID for the call.

name.description: Conversation name (auto-generated or user-provided).

status.description: The status of the conversation. `in_progress` means the session is active, `ended` means it completed successfully, `failed` means it ended due to an error.

avatar.description: The avatar used in this conversation, or null if unavailable.

anyOf.description: The avatar used in this conversation.

oneOf.title: PresetAvatarSummary
oneOf.description: A preset avatar.

name.description: The preset avatar's display name (e.g. "Mina").

oneOf.title: CustomAvatarSummary
oneOf.description: A custom avatar created by the user.

id.description: The custom avatar ID, or null if deleted.

name.description: The avatar's configured name, or null if unavailable.

duration.description: Duration of the conversation in seconds, or null if not started.

hasTools.description: Whether tools were configured for this conversation session.

hasMore.description: Whether there are more conversations to fetch.

nextCursor.description: Cursor to use for fetching the next page, or null if no more pages.

get.description: Get aggregate usage statistics for avatar conversations, including total duration, session counts, average duration, and a per-day breakdown. Per-day buckets are keyed by UTC calendar date. The date range must not exceed 90 days.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start of the date range in UTC (inclusive). Required.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End of the date range in UTC (exclusive). Required.

totalSeconds.description: Total seconds across conversations with a measured duration in the date range.

totalSessions.description: Number of conversations started in the date range. Includes unfinished and failed conversations.

avgDurationSeconds.description: Average duration in seconds across conversations with a measured duration, or 0 if none completed. May not equal `totalSeconds / totalSessions` because unfinished conversations contribute to the session count but not the duration.

byDay.description: Per-day usage across the date range. Days with no sessions are included with zeroes.

sessions.description: Number of conversations started on this date.

seconds.description: Total seconds of measured conversation duration on this date.

get.description: Get detailed information about a specific conversation, including the transcript and recording download URL when available. The conversation ID is the same value returned when the realtime session was created.

  ratio='1280:720',
  duration=8,
).wait_for_task_output()

print(task)

  ratio='1280:720',
).wait_for_task_output()

print(task)

# pip install runwayml
from runwayml import RunwayML

# The env var RUNWAYML_API_SECRET is expected to contain your API key.
client = RunwayML()

# Run a published workflow with custom inputs
invocation = client.workflows.run(
  'YOUR_WORKFLOW_ID',
  node_outputs={
    'node-uuid': {
      # For text/number/boolean values, use type: 'primitive'
      'prompt': { 'type': 'primitive', 'value': 'A beautiful sunset' },
      # For media assets, use type: 'image', 'video', or 'audio'
      'image': { 'type': 'image', 'uri': 'https://example.com/image.jpg' },
    },
  },
)

# Then poll for the workflow result
invocation = client.workflow_invocations.retrieve(id=invocation.id).wait_for_task_output()
print(invocation)
